arxiv_papers.title: Towards imaging Earth's large-scale structures by directional geoneutrino detection with Ocean Bottom Detector
arxiv_papers.snippet: Geoneutrinos, electron antineutrinos produced by radioactive decays of heat-producing elements (HPEs) within the Earth, provide unique insights into Earth's interior and heat budget since their first detection in 2005 by KamLAND. youtube_items.summary: So the planetary boundary science now has 50 years of scientific advancement and is based on three fundamental pieces of evidence that has been advancing over the past 30 years. And the first one is that we've entered a whole new geological epoch, the Anthropocene. Anthros, Greek for humans. We're now, as humanity, the world as we know it, the dominating force of change on planet Earth. The scale, the speed, and the connectivity has reached the point where we are starting to see the first indications of hitting the ceiling of hardwired biophysical processes that regulates the stability, the resilience, and the life support on Earth. The evidence for this comes not from models, not from theories, but from observations. It's the great acceleration curves that you see here. They're now iconic

youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:ndPVcg6uSZc
youtube_items.summary: Welcome to the 2025 planetary health check report by the planetary boundary science lab here at the potam institute for climate impact research. The 2025 report confirms the scientific consensus that we have a deep planetary challenge. Six of the nine planetary boundaries were breached and this year we're forced to conclude that a seventh boundary also has been breached. It is ocean acidification that is now also moving into the risk zone. All of these seven boundaries are unfortunately continuing to move in the wrong direction. So we haven't yet bent the curves towards the safe landing for humanity within the safe operating space of the planetary boundaries on Earth. This is the major challenge. We're not yet in the high-risk zone of irreversible, unmanageable change of the planet, which
